title: Admin Login Visibility and Rupee Currency Display

requirements.text: On the LoginPage, ensure the admin login option is clearly visible and accessible. The toggle or button to switch to 'admin' login mode must be displayed prominently on the page without requiring any hidden interaction or URL parameter manipulation.
acceptanceCriteria: A clearly labeled 'Admin Login' button or tab is visible on the login page without any special URL parameters
Clicking the admin login toggle switches the form to admin mode
Admin login mode is visually distinguishable from user login mode

requirements.text: On the LoginPage, display a currency note or label showing the Indian Rupee symbol (₹) instead of or alongside the US Dollar symbol ($). Any pricing or currency references on the login page must use ₹ (INR) formatting.

quotes: Currency display ($ to ₹) on the login page

acceptanceCriteria: No bare $ symbol appears on the login page
Any currency value displayed uses the ₹ (Indian Rupee) symbol
Currency display is consistent and correctly formatted

requirements.text: Ensure all login flows (both user and admin) correctly persist session/authentication state after login so that the user remains logged in on navigation. Post-login redirects must work: admin users go to the admin dashboard, registered users go to the user dashboard, and unregistered users go to the registration page.

quotes: make sure to save changes and loginns

acceptanceCriteria: After a successful user login, authenticated state is preserved and the user is redirected to the correct page
After a successful admin login, the admin is redirected to the admin dashboard
Newly authenticated users without a profile are redirected to the registration page
Refreshing the page after login does not log the user out

constraints: Do not modify files under frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui
Keep all Motoko logic in backend/main.mo — do not create additional backend files unless migration is required
nonGoals: Adding any new backend endpoints or data models
Changing the overall app theme or design language
Implementing a custom currency conversion API or real-time exchange rates
